approach: the fold-back is a MOVE, not a duplicate, and the requester pre-authorized the Clearance it fires. mux/ becomes the sole owner of what from/ratio/env mean at the seam; the seam-convention scenarios LEAVE layout.feature. The alternative (add at mux, leave layout alone) is purely additive and needs no clearance, but it manufactures the exact defect cross-node overlap detection exists to catch: one behavior specified in two nodes makes a change to it touch both files, so two file-disjoint-looking missions are a hard collision the scenario rung cannot see.

WHICH node owns the fact is settled by the suite bar's test-vector rule rather than by preference. layout.feature's ratio outline says 'Given a split node with ratio 0.333 applied through the <backend> adapter'; swap the template domain for a direct open() carrying a ratio and the Then still holds, so the template framing is APPARATUS and the precondition is a pane:* open carrying a ratio. The scenario is a mux-layer fact wearing a layout costume.

scope is spec-only, requester-ratified: no behavior change, no --ratio/--env CLI flags, no changeset. describeRegion — the other seam member specified through layout under the same house style — is deliberately out; #10 names only the three split fields.

BLOCKER carried in, not scope creep: mux.feature has NEVER parsed as Gherkin. An Examples table declares | branch | placement | and two of its three rows carry one cell, so the whole file is rejected — its entire frozen suite is invisible to check-suite and binds to nothing. The spec gate runs check-suite fail-closed over touched suites, so this CR cannot gate without the repair. Repairing it takes the node from 0 parsed / 0 bound to 61 scenarios / 34 bound / 34 pass / 0 fail / 27 unbound. The malformed outline asserts NOTHING today, so the repair widens the contract and narrows nothing — the freeze self-clears.

a stale doc comment is corrected rather than shipped: session.ts says env is 'only meaningful for a pane:* placement' while both backends emit it at every tier and the tests pin exactly that. Load-bearing, because a layout's root pane is born by the region open and never by a split, so tier-scoping env would drop it silently. Comment-only, no behavior.

the out-of-range ratio is deliberately NOT specified. The seam renders whatever it is given (open({ratio: 1.5}) emits -l -50%); the range bar lives only in the layout schema. Freezing today's behavior into a scenario would cement a wart and block a fix, so it is a prose boundary in the README plus a follow-up.

binding is a RENAME job, not new coverage. The bridge binds a test only when its leaf title is the scenario name verbatim; the adapter tests already sit under the spec:cyber-mux/mux wrapper and already cover the behavior, but their titles are implementation-voiced and bind to nothing. One genuine gap gets a new test: ratio's suppression for tab/workspace on tmux rests on a !window guard no test exercises.

why: cold sdd-spec-judge (fresh context, independent) judged the revised mux/ node — README use cases + behavior-table row + 11 scenarios added to the frozen mux.feature — and the paired layout/ narrowing, backward against {oracle,builder,architect}: all PASS, ALIGNED true, zero failing scenarios, no blocker, no open markers. Mechanical check-suite + check-spec-state green. It re-derived the edit classification structurally rather than trusting the brief (gherkin-cli diff --base HEAD mux.feature: 11 added / 0 modified / 0 removed, addOnly true, so the freeze self-clears; --base origin/main layout.feature: 0 added / 0 modified / 3 removed, exactly the scenarios claimed to move). It verified EVERY backend claim against the adapter source rather than this CR's prose — herdr's pane id is positional and no --from flag exists, toTmuxSize(0.333) really is 67%, the !window guards, env emitted at every tier on both backends, herdr's worktree create/open accepting env in their type and never referencing it, canSizeSplits true on both. It ran the miss test over all 11 new scenarios and found none inert, and ran the absorption check in the direction that threatened this CR (scenarios authored AFTER reading the implementation): no apparatus leaked into a Given, and it established that the flag literals are not a new style but the same literals layout.feature already carried. It checked pairwise consistency across all 72 scenarios and found no contradiction. Two findings worth recording. FIRST, it independently confirmed the load-bearing blocker rather than taking it on trust: it stashed the working tree and re-ran the differ against the committed baseline, reproducing EPARSE — origin/main's mux.feature genuinely does not parse, so the entire frozen suite was invisible and bound to nothing, and the repair (242ce5d) widens a scenario that asserted nothing. SECOND, it checked the lifecycle carve-out this CR could have abused: the pure-move/rename exemption (zero content delta preserves freeze) does NOT apply here because the moved scenarios were reworded, and it confirmed this CR does not claim it — the narrowing is named as a Clearance that the requester pre-authorized at intake. Three non-blocking observations carried forward: the README's no-validation boundary has no scenario by design (D4, to avoid freezing a wart, filed as a follow-up); the ratio-is-a-split-concept scenario is tmux-only because herdr's size variable is lexically scoped inside the pane-split branch and cannot leak; and canSizeSplits' silence-means-cannot reading is documented at mux while the consuming degrade lives in layout, which does not distinguish undefined from explicit false. Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash (by user) — ratify or kick back.
